blocks.html_en: Isn't SQLite's source code in its training data?

sentences.en: Isn't SQLite's source code in its training data?
sentences.zh: SQLite的源代码不是在它的训练数据中吗？

blocks.html_en: Even if no Rust code for it was seen during training, an LLM can trivially transpile SQLite's C codebase to Rust on the fly. For example, I just asked ChatGPT to write John Carmack's famous Fast Inverse Square Root algorithm in Erlang, without searching online or thinking, and it transpiled it immediately (while also extracting the knowledge in the same step). SQLite's semantics/code are stored in the middle layers of an LLM, and the last layers are able to convert it into any representation, as conditioned by the prompt. Cursor's experiment is deeply flawed because they merely extracted the model's compressed, lossy knowledge of SQLite's codebase and then just ran a bunch of tests/fixing rounds to make up for the lossiness. The claim that the agents built it from scratch is false.

sentences.en: Even if no Rust code for it was seen during training, an LLM can trivially transpile SQLite's C codebase to Rust on the fly.
sentences.zh: 即使在训练期间没有看到它的 Rust 代码，LLM 也可以轻松地将 SQLite 的 C 代码库即时转译为 Rust。

sentences.en: For example, I just asked ChatGPT to write John Carmack's famous Fast Inverse Square Root algorithm in Erlang, without searching online or thinking, and it transpiled it immediately (while also extracting the knowledge in the same step).
sentences.zh: 例如，我只是要求 ChatGPT 用 Erlang 编写 John Carmack 著名的快速反平方根算法，没有在网上搜索或思考，它立即转译了它（同时也在同一步骤中提取了知识）。

sentences.en: SQLite's semantics/code are stored in the middle layers of an LLM, and the last layers are able to convert it into any representation, as conditioned by the prompt.
sentences.zh: SQLite 的语义/代码存储在 LLM 的中间层中，最后一层能够根据提示将其转换为任何表示形式。

sentences.en: Cursor's experiment is deeply flawed because they merely extracted the model's compressed, lossy knowledge of SQLite's codebase and then just ran a bunch of tests/fixing rounds to make up for the lossiness.
sentences.zh: Cursor 的实验存在严重缺陷，因为他们只是提取了模型的 SQLite 代码库的压缩、有损知识，然后运行了一系列测试/修复轮次来弥补损失。

sentences.en: The claim that the agents built it from scratch is false.
sentences.zh: 关于特工从头开始建造它的说法是错误的。

blocks.html_en: Even if no Rust code for it was seen during training, an LLM can trivially transpile SQLite's C codebase to Rust on the fly. For example, I just asked ChatGPT to write John Carmack's famous Fast Inverse Square Root algorithm in Erlang, without searching online or thinking, and it transpiled it immediately (while also extracting the knowledge in the same step). SQLite's semantics/code are stored in the middle layers of an LLM, and the last layers are able to convert it into any representation, as conditioned by the prompt. Cursor's experiment is deeply flawed because they merely extracted the model's compressed, lossy knowledge of SQLite's codebase and then just ran a bunch of tests/fixing rounds to make up for the lossiness. The claim that the agents built it from scratch is false.

sentences.en: Even if no Rust code for it was seen during training, an LLM can trivially transpile SQLite's C codebase to Rust on the fly.
sentences.zh: 即使在训练期间没有看到它的 Rust 代码，LLM 也可以轻松地将 SQLite 的 C 代码库即时转译为 Rust。

sentences.zh: SQLite 的语义/代码存储在 LLM 的中间层中，最后一层能够根据提示将其转换为任何表示形式。

blocks.html_en:
The point is that the model has the source code in its weights. Therefore it's misleading to focus on being "only given the documentation", when it could produce a SQLite implementation in rust with no documentation at all. The wording is designed to create a marketing impression that it can do spec-driven development for a novel project, but this example doesn't actually support that.

It's like a diet system advertising how much weight a spokesperson lost on their system, without disclosing that they also went on Ozempic at the same time.

sentences.zh: 关键是模型的权重中有源代码。

sentences.zh: 因此，当它可以在 Rust 中生成 SQLite 实现而不需要任何文档时，专注于“仅给出文档”是具有误导性的。

blocks.html_en:
Why tho? The whole point of agentic is that it takes several passes to get to a result. If step a) is a non-idiomatic transpile, but step b) c) and ... z) make it idiomatic and performant and efficient, why look at step a)? That was just the beginning. The idea with "agents" and "swarms" and dynamic this and that is that you only look at the last step.

This strictly follows the early agentic usage, where people were complaining of code full of errors and such. But having the loop do plan-write-verify is the thing that got us from that state to today. And it works. If you look at the code and it has errors (linting, etc) then your loop is not properly setup. You should only be called upon when all that can and should pass does. Your role is "direction/taste/human-common-sense-at-a-glance".

sentences.zh: 为什么呢？

sentences.en: The whole point of agentic is that it takes several passes to get to a result.
sentences.zh: 代理的全部要点是需要多次传递才能得到结果。

sentences.en: If step a) is a non-idiomatic transpile, but step b) c) and ...
sentences.zh: 如果步骤 a) 是非惯用的转译，但步骤 b) c) 和...

sentences.en: z) make it idiomatic and performant and efficient, why look at step a)?
sentences.zh: z) 使其变得惯用、高效且高效，为什么要考虑步骤 a)？

sentences.zh: 那只是开始。

sentences.en: But having the loop do plan-write-verify is the thing that got us from that state to today.
sentences.zh: 但是让循环执行计划-写入-验证是让我们从那种状态发展到今天的原因。

sentences.en: And it works.
sentences.zh: 它有效。

sentences.zh: 如果您查看代码并发现其中有错误（linting 等），则说明您的循环设置不正确。

sentences.en: Your role is "direction/taste/human-common-sense-at-a-glance".
sentences.zh: 你的角色是“方向/品味/人类常识一览”。

blocks.html_en: Because the point of the test is to see what the agentic swarm approach is capable of. If your test assumes that a version of the thing you are trying to build is available in the training data, or can't determine if it matters whether the thing you're building is in the training data, then it's not a very useful test. Most of the time when you consider agentic approaches, you're not trying to rebuild something the agents involved are trained on.

sentences.zh: 因为测试的目的是看看代理群体方法的能力。

sentences.en: If your test assumes that a version of the thing you are trying to build is available in the training data, or can't determine if it matters whether the thing you're building is in the training data, then it's not a very useful test.
sentences.zh: 如果您的测试假设您尝试构建的内容的版本在训练数据中可用，或者无法确定您正在构建的内容是否在训练数据中是否重要，那么这不是一个非常有用的测试。

sentences.en: Most of the time when you consider agentic approaches, you're not trying to rebuild something the agents involved are trained on.
sentences.zh: 大多数时候，当您考虑代理方法时，您并不是试图重建所涉及的代理所接受的培训。

blocks.html_en:
These tests are interesting but I can't help feeling that these are benchmaxxing results. "We made our harness make sqlite in rust" sounds like a much easier probelm statement than having to recreate an application like facebook. I think the concept of having to integrate is the real challenge. Re-writing code in new languages based on decent docs is a difficult but less useful measure of how AI can help replace engineers.

Imagine this: you need to build a new CRUD app but your AI agent spends 20M toks on just writing it's own custom login function, that's a waste of time and money. I would not like to have a new system developed for me in such cases. I think we need a test where the agents are more specifically tasked to find technologies worth integrating and hence write the fewest lines of code.

sentences.en: These tests are interesting but I can't help feeling that these are benchmaxxing results.
sentences.zh: 这些测试很有趣，但我不禁觉得这些都是基准测试结果。

sentences.en: "We made our harness make sqlite in rust" sounds like a much easier probelm statement than having to recreate an application like facebook.
sentences.zh: “我们让我们的工具在 Rust 中使用 sqlite”听起来比重新创建像 facebook 这样的应用程序要容易得多。

sentences.en: I think the concept of having to integrate is the real challenge.
sentences.zh: 我认为必须整合的概念才是真正的挑战。

sentences.en: Re-writing code in new languages based on decent docs is a difficult but less useful measure of how AI can help replace engineers.Imagine this: you need to build a new CRUD app but your AI agent spends 20M toks on just writing it's own custom login function, that's a waste of time and money.
sentences.zh: 基于体面的文档用新语言重写代码是一个困难但不太有用的衡量人工智能如何帮助取代工程师的方法。想象一下：你需要构建一个新的 CRUD 应用程序，但你的人工智能代理花费 2000 万托克来编写它自己的自定义登录功能，这是浪费时间和金钱。

sentences.en: I would not like to have a new system developed for me in such cases.
sentences.zh: 在这种情况下，我不希望有人为我开发新系统。

sentences.en: I think we need a test where the agents are more specifically tasked to find technologies worth integrating and hence write the fewest lines of code.
sentences.zh: 我认为我们需要一个测试，其中代理的任务更具体，以找到值得集成的技术，从而编写最少的代码行。

blocks.html_en: The nature of ML is to reach for easily measured proxy problems which can then be optimized. Why worry about a backoffice integrated micro-service you have no data on when you aren't sure that you can design and implement a rewrite of an OSS project?

sentences.zh: 机器学习的本质是解决易于测量的代理问题，然后对其进行优化。

sentences.en: Why worry about a backoffice integrated micro-service you have no data on when you aren't sure that you can design and implement a rewrite of an OSS project?
sentences.zh: 当您不确定是否可以设计和实现 OSS 项目的重写时，为什么还要担心没有数据的后台集成微服务呢？

blocks.html_en:
How do we know if these models weren’t trained on Turso’s rewrite of SQLite in Rust?

It seems both likely that they were and impossible to remove that code from pretraining. Doesn’t that make this just about LLM memorization of the training set? What am I missing?

sentences.en: How do we know if these models weren’t trained on Turso’s rewrite of SQLite in Rust?It seems both likely that they were and impossible to remove that code from pretraining.
sentences.zh: 我们如何知道这些模型是否没有经过 Turso 用 Rust 重写 SQLite 进行训练？它们似乎很可能并且不可能从预训练中删除该代码。

sentences.en: Doesn’t that make this just about LLM memorization of the training set?
sentences.zh: 这难道不就是LLM对训练集的记忆吗？

sentences.en: What am I missing?
sentences.zh: 我缺少什么？

> What am I missing?

They’re testing the same models on the same task but different ways of organising the swarms and the new approach works better.

I would have loved to see more of the harness engineering shared as code. Instead we are left with only the outcome.

I guess that makes sense since the harness is the product in the case of Cursor.

blocks.html_en:
I call this junk "meta-agentic engineering", it reminds me of people who have the coolest nvim configs, spend hundreds of hours customizing it but ultimately get less work done than the guy with minimal workflows, if any at all.

I look on twitter and it's just people building tools for agents to use agents, some weird customization loop going on in the LLM space right now. Ultimately these are trends pushed on us from model providers because they 10x token consumption. Its literally just BS trends to increase revenue at these companies, most of it is largely useless.

sentences.en: I call this junk "meta-agentic engineering", it reminds me of people who have the coolest nvim configs, spend hundreds of hours customizing it but ultimately get less work done than the guy with minimal workflows, if any at all.I look on twitter and it's just people building tools for agents to use agents, some weird customization loop going on in the LLM space right now.
sentences.zh: 我把这个垃圾称为“元代理工程”，它让我想起那些拥有最酷的 nvim 配置的人，花费数百小时对其进行定制，但最终比那些工作流程最少的人完成的工作更少（如果有的话）。我在 Twitter 上查看，这只是人们为代理构建工具来使用代理，现在 LLM 领域正在发生一些奇怪的定制循环。

sentences.en: Ultimately these are trends pushed on us from model providers because they 10x token consumption.
sentences.zh: 最终，这些是模型提供商向我们推动的趋势，因为他们的代币消耗量是 10 倍。

sentences.en: Its literally just BS trends to increase revenue at these companies, most of it is largely useless.
sentences.zh: 这实际上只是这些公司增加收入的胡言乱语，其中大部分基本上是无用的。

blocks.html_en: Some of this stuff is ludicrous. I finally tried /loop last week and discovered every loop iteration passes the entire context history to the model. So pretty quickly you're running a full 1M context window, without cache, likely just to check if something is ready or needs to be done. It's miserably terrible engineering unless your entire and only goal is to burn tokens.

sentences.en: Some of this stuff is ludicrous.
sentences.zh: 其中有些东西很荒谬。

sentences.en: I finally tried /loop last week and discovered every loop iteration passes the entire context history to the model.
sentences.zh: 我上周终于尝试了 /loop 并发现每次循环迭代都会将整个上下文历史记录传递给模型。

sentences.en: So pretty quickly you're running a full 1M context window, without cache, likely just to check if something is ready or needs to be done.
sentences.zh: 因此很快您就会运行一个完整的 1M 上下文窗口，没有缓存，可能只是为了检查某些内容是否已准备好或需要完成。

sentences.en: It's miserably terrible engineering unless your entire and only goal is to burn tokens.
sentences.zh: 除非你的全部且唯一的目标是销毁代币，否则这是非常糟糕的工程。

sentences.en: I finally tried /loop last week and discovered every loop iteration passes the entire context history to the model.
sentences.zh: 我上周终于尝试了 /loop 并发现每次循环迭代都会将整个上下文历史记录传递给模型。

sentences.en: So pretty quickly you're running a full 1M context window, without cache, likely just to check if something is ready or needs to be done.
sentences.zh: 因此很快您就会运行一个完整的 1M 上下文窗口，没有缓存，可能只是为了检查某些内容是否已准备好或需要完成。

sentences.en: It's miserably terrible engineering unless your entire and only goal is to burn tokens.
sentences.zh: 除非你的全部且唯一的目标是销毁代币，否则这是非常糟糕的工程。

blocks.html_en: Because it's typically cached for 5min (1hr is a setting you have to explicitly opt into), and very few people run loops at a cadence of < 5 mins.

blocks.html_en:
https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching#on-a-claude-s...

"On a Claude subscription, Claude Code requests the one-hour TTL automatically. Usage is included in your plan rather than billed per token, so the longer TTL costs you nothing extra and only affects how long your cache stays warm. If you’ve gone over your plan’s usage limit and Claude Code is drawing on usage credits, you are billed for that usage, so Claude Code automatically drops the TTL to five minutes."

blocks.html_en: I think many of us engineers (including myself) are suffering from AI-induced procrastination. Fixing that pesky bug in production isn't fun. Building a harness/workflow that detects and fixes pesky bugs in production on its own is way more fun (especially when your employer pays for tokens), and it feels just as important, if not more! So what if it didn't actually fix that pesky bug in production on its own...

sentences.en: I think many of us engineers (including myself) are suffering from AI-induced procrastination.
sentences.zh: 我认为我们许多工程师（包括我自己）都患有人工智能引起的拖延症。

sentences.en: Fixing that pesky bug in production isn't fun.
sentences.zh: 修复生产中那个讨厌的错误并不有趣。

sentences.en: Building a harness/workflow that detects and fixes pesky bugs in production on its own is way more fun (especially when your employer pays for tokens), and it feels just as important, if not more!
sentences.zh: 构建一个能够自行检测和修复生产中讨厌的错误的工具/工作流程会更有趣（特别是当您的雇主支付代币费用时），而且感觉同样重要，甚至更重要！

sentences.en: So what if it didn't actually fix that pesky bug in production on its own...
sentences.zh: 那么，如果它本身没有真正修复生产中那个讨厌的错误怎么办......

blocks.html_en:
I don't think there is a lot test harness, given there is a golden implementation (sqlite3), with clear interface (SQL, and sqlite3 db files). Once you slop crappy implementation, then you can create one group of agents finding gap where slop doing wrong vs golden, and another group of agents filling the gap meeting the conformance.

I assume they did similar, as long as test case(or spec) can be parallelized. Having parallel execution / testing environment is crucial if we need 1000 commits/second throughput.

I would not surprised they have 1+ millions of specs.

I would bet they won't release generated specs - actually this will be a valuable asset like https://sqlite.org/th3.html

sentences.en: I don't think there is a lot test harness, given there is a golden implementation (sqlite3), with clear interface (SQL, and sqlite3 db files).
sentences.zh: 我不认为有很多测试工具，因为有一个黄金实现（sqlite3），具有清晰的接口（SQL 和 sqlite3 数据库文件）。

sentences.en: Once you slop crappy implementation, then you can create one group of agents finding gap where slop doing wrong vs golden, and another group of agents filling the gap meeting the conformance.I assume they did similar, as long as test case(or spec) can be parallelized.
sentences.zh: 一旦你搞砸了糟糕的实施，那么你可以创建一组代理来寻找差距，其中的差距是错误的，而另一组代理则填补了满足一致性的差距。我认为他们做了类似的事情，只要测试用例（或规范）可以并行化。

sentences.en: Having parallel execution / testing environment is crucial if we need 1000 commits/second throughput.I would not surprised they have 1+ millions of specs.I would bet they won't release generated specs - actually this will be a valuable asset like https://sqlite.org/th3.html
sentences.zh: 如果我们需要 1000 次提交/秒吞吐量，拥有并行执行/测试环境至关重要。他们拥有 1+ 数百万个规格我不会感到惊讶。我敢打赌他们不会发布生成的规格 - 实际上这将是一个宝贵的资产，如 https://sqlite.org/th3.html

blocks.html_en: I initially thought that getting agents to work for longer and in large groups was the future, but I'm increasingly thinking that, at least for engineering, just one thread makes more sense. The agent pulls things into context as needed. One thing that I've been experimenting with is also letting the agent remove things from context, such as files. But just adding to the context and compacting when it's full seems like it might beat a lot of more advanced options. Because the model is good, it knows what to put in the summary; just enough for the model to be able to rebuild the context from that seed (e.g. pulling in relevant files/data into the context).

sentences.en: I initially thought that getting agents to work for longer and in large groups was the future, but I'm increasingly thinking that, at least for engineering, just one thread makes more sense.
sentences.zh: 我最初认为让代理工作更长时间并在大型团体中工作是未来的趋势，但我越来越认为，至少对于工程来说，只有一个线程更有意义。

sentences.en: The agent pulls things into context as needed.
sentences.zh: 代理根据需要将事物拉入上下文中。

sentences.en: One thing that I've been experimenting with is also letting the agent remove things from context, such as files.
sentences.zh: 我一直在尝试的一件事是让代理从上下文中删除内容，例如文件。

sentences.en: But just adding to the context and compacting when it's full seems like it might beat a lot of more advanced options.
sentences.zh: 但仅仅添加到上下文并在其满时进行压缩似乎可能会击败许多更高级的选项。

sentences.en: Because the model is good, it knows what to put in the summary; just enough for the model to be able to rebuild the context from that seed (e.g. pulling in relevant files/data into the context).
sentences.zh: 因为模型好，它知道摘要中应该放什么；足以让模型能够从该种子重建上下文（例如，将相关文件/数据拉入上下文中）。

blocks.html_en:
I think experiments like these are exciting, but they only work when you have the full suite against which you calibrate.

What I'd love to see is a small group of humans steering a million agents.

sentences.en: I think experiments like these are exciting, but they only work when you have the full suite against which you calibrate.What I'd love to see is a small group of humans steering a million agents.

blocks.html_en:
If you can't see cases for it you may need some glasses. Replace the graph in the docs of Top-level planner -> mid-level planners -> worker leaf nodes and you get an org chart of CEO -> executives, N-levels of management, and ICs at the leafs. It directly maps to almost any office-based company that can operate completely digitally.

It's obviously in its infancy but fully agentic companies are becoming a possibility, given the right harness, models and tools

sentences.zh: 如果您看不到它的外壳，您可能需要一副眼镜。

sentences.zh: 替换文档中的顶层规划者 -> 中层规划者 -> Worker 叶子节点的图表，您将得到 CEO -> 高管、N 级管理人员和叶子 IC 的组织结构图。

blocks.html_en:
I don't think Gas Town was actually all that much ahead of the curve - see Cursor's swarm post from January this year, mentioned at the very top of this blog post.

I also don't know that beads is a very strong example that Yegge knows what he's doing - it's famously been described as (pseudo)malware.

sentences.zh: 我认为 Gas Town 实际上并没有那么领先 - 请参阅今年 1 月 Cursor 的 swarm 帖子，在本博客文章的最顶部提到。我也不知道珠子是一个非常有力的例子，表明 Yegge 知道他在做什么 - 众所周知，它被描述为（伪）恶意软件。

blocks.html_en:
Even at a much smaller scale of a few dozen parallel agents working I've found real benefit to a structured hierarchy of agents. Not just keeping the context clean and focused at the right level, but if you find that something has just gone off the rails, you can more easily rip out or even just fully delete a leg of work without damaging the original design. It's also nice when you find a design change is needed and you can communicate that to the top level and have it efficiently propagate down where it matters.

I like it so much I even recorded a video walking through the process!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=efUmcCiRoDU (I do walk through how I do this in our app DevSwarm here)

sentences.en: Even at a much smaller scale of a few dozen parallel agents working I've found real benefit to a structured hierarchy of agents.
sentences.zh: 即使在规模小得多的几十个并行代理工作的情况下，我也发现代理的结构化层次结构具有真正的好处。

sentences.en: Not just keeping the context clean and focused at the right level, but if you find that something has just gone off the rails, you can more easily rip out or even just fully delete a leg of work without damaging the original design.
sentences.zh: 不仅要保持上下文干净并集中在正确的水平上，而且如果您发现某些内容刚刚偏离轨道，您可以更轻松地撕掉甚至完全删除一部分工作，而不会损坏原始设计。

sentences.en: It's also nice when you find a design change is needed and you can communicate that to the top level and have it efficiently propagate down where it matters.I like it so much I even recorded a video walking through the process!https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=efUmcCiRoDU (I do walk through how I do this in our app DevSwarm here)
sentences.zh: 当您发现需要进行设计更改时，您可以将其传达给顶层并使其有效地传播到重要的地方，这也很好。我非常喜欢它，我什至录制了一个演示整个过程的视频！https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=efUmcCiRoDU（我在这里演示了如何在我们的应用程序 DevSwarm 中执行此操作）

blocks.html_en: Kudos

This is super fascinating, and I loved seeing testing of where exactly you need frontier intelligence -- looks like coordination / planning, but not coding right now -- the article's a bit of a tease, as we can't play with such a harness, or their new version control system or get a workable artifact out of it.

That said, I love the work on figuring out these harness coordination jobs. While there are analogs to human management there is also this enticing feeling that, since the models are broadly deterministic, we might be able to get repeatable science-type lessons about managing them with enough testing.

sentences.en: This is super fascinating, and I loved seeing testing of where exactly you need frontier intelligence -- looks like coordination / planning, but not coding right now -- the article's a bit of a tease, as we can't play with such a harness, or their new version control system or get a workable artifact out of it.That said, I love the work on figuring out these harness coordination jobs.
sentences.zh: 这太令人着迷了，我喜欢看到测试你到底需要前沿智能的地方——看起来像协调/规划，但现在不是编码——这篇文章有点戏弄，因为我们不能使用这样的工具，或者他们的新版本控制系统，或者从中得到一个可行的工件。也就是说，我喜欢弄清楚这些工具协调工作的工作。

sentences.en: While there are analogs to human management there is also this enticing feeling that, since the models are broadly deterministic, we might be able to get repeatable science-type lessons about managing them with enough testing.
sentences.zh: 虽然与人类管理有类似之处，但也有一种诱人的感觉，即由于模型具有广泛的确定性，我们也许能够通过足够的测试获得有关管理它们的可重复的科学类型课程。

blocks.html_en:
This post describes ideas found in existing agent orchestrator systems since they've existed. You'll find the same "top level agent that breaks a problem down into spec / plan / implement / verify" pattern in get-shit-done or superpowers.

Is the original part the VCS that works at 1000 commits/second? Or spending a bunch of money comparing models?

blocks.html_en: Anything architectural like this is super interesting. We keep getting more powerful models sure. We're getting agents ok. But this coordination of many agents. This is the part that's really going to scale very fast and across many many machines in parallel. Very cool.

sentences.zh: 我们找代理没问题。

sentences.en: But this coordination of many agents.
sentences.zh: 但这需要很多代理商的协调。

sentences.en: This is the part that's really going to scale very fast and across many many machines in parallel.
sentences.zh: 这是真正能够快速扩展并跨许多台机器并行扩展的部分。

sentences.en: Very cool.
sentences.zh: 非常酷。

blocks.html_en:
I find these blog posts (and the originals, with Anthropic's C compiler and Cursor's browser) somewhat funny, as if they have this enormous power to build ... but they can't build something unique or new. Like the software sucks, but look how powerful the process is (the models are indeed powerful).

And it's a bit of a shame: by virtue of their position (their embedding in the fabric of venture capitalism), it seems like they can only make a subset of things -- what they can make is dictated enormously by capital, as they are engines of capital.

Not sure the point I'm trying to make, I just find it amusing.

Perhaps the point is that it might be more worthwhile to give independent creators a billion dollars to play around with agent swarms if we want to keep diversity in the evolutionary algorithm that is the software industry high.

sentences.en: I find these blog posts (and the originals, with Anthropic's C compiler and Cursor's browser) somewhat funny, as if they have this enormous power to build ...
sentences.zh: 我发现这些博客文章（以及原版，使用 Anthropic 的 C 编译器和 Cursor 的浏览器）有点有趣，就好像它们有巨大的能力来构建......

sentences.en: but they can't build something unique or new.
sentences.zh: 但他们无法创造出独特或新颖的东西。

sentences.en: Like the software sucks, but look how powerful the process is (the models are indeed powerful).And it's a bit of a shame: by virtue of their position (their embedding in the fabric of venture capitalism), it seems like they can only make a subset of things -- what they can make is dictated enormously by capital, as they are engines of capital.Not sure the point I'm trying to make, I just find it amusing.Perhaps the point is that it might be more worthwhile to give independent creators a billion dollars to play around with agent swarms if we want to keep diversity in the evolutionary algorithm that is the software industry high.
sentences.zh: 就像软件很糟糕，但看看这个过程有多强大（模型确实强大）。这有点遗憾：凭借他们的地位（他们嵌入风险资本主义的结构），似乎他们只能制造一部分东西——他们能制造的东西在很大程度上是由资本决定的，因为它们是资本的引擎。不确定我想表达的观点，我只是觉得这很有趣。也许重点是，给予独立的可能更值得如果我们想保持软件行业的进化算法的多样性，那么创造者就需要花费十亿美元来研究代理群。

blocks.html_en:
Because it's mostly a load of crap.

Most of these reusable, automated workflows sound really cool in theory, but in practice either they require a lot of customization (not really reusable) or a lot of babysitting (not really automated).

AI has improved my productivity significantly, I'm not a hater, but we also have to be honest about its (current) limitations. The whole "software factory" thing is just a pipe dream people sell to execs / investors / online courses.

sentences.en: Because it's mostly a load of crap.Most of these reusable, automated workflows sound really cool in theory, but in practice either they require a lot of customization (not really reusable) or a lot of babysitting (not really automated).AI has improved my productivity significantly, I'm not a hater, but we also have to be honest about its (current) limitations.
sentences.zh: 因为它主要是一堆废话。大多数这些可重用的自动化工作流程在理论上听起来真的很酷，但在实践中，它们要么需要大量定制（不是真正可重用），要么需要大量保姆（不是真正自动化）。人工智能显着提高了我的生产力，我不是一个讨厌的人，但我们也必须诚实地对待它（当前）的局限性。

sentences.en: The whole "software factory" thing is just a pipe dream people sell to execs / investors / online courses.
sentences.zh: 整个“软件工厂”只是人们向高管/投资者/在线课程推销的白日梦。

blocks.html_en:
Yes and this can be proven because what Anthropic says and does is vastly different

They say SaaS is dead and you can vibe code any SaaS using Opus and yet despite having Mythos with them they have to use JS terminal to run Claude Code.

Why don't they just vibe code CC? Why doesn't Dario have a 100000 agent swarm to run entirety of Anthropic or at least software department?

sentences.en: Yes and this can be proven because what Anthropic says and does is vastly differentThey say SaaS is dead and you can vibe code any SaaS using Opus and yet despite having Mythos with them they have to use JS terminal to run Claude Code.Why don't they just vibe code CC?
sentences.zh: 是的，这一点可以得到证明，因为 Anthropic 说的和做的有很大不同。他们说 SaaS 已经死了，你可以使用 Opus 来振动任何 SaaS 代码，但尽管他们有 Mythos，他们必须使用 JS 终端来运行 Claude Code。为什么他们不直接振动代码 CC？

blocks.html_en:
My app is written in TypeScript. With agents I could rewrite it in Rust, I just don’t need to so I don’t. It doesn’t warrant that level of performance and the business logic is more digestible in its current form. Given the amount of reviewing I need to do now, readability is worth more than performance. There are always tradeoffs.

Are you implying that them attempting a Rust rewrite would fail, and this is why they’re not doing it?

sentences.en: My app is written in TypeScript.
sentences.zh: 我的应用程序是用 TypeScript 编写的。

sentences.en: With agents I could rewrite it in Rust, I just don’t need to so I don’t.
sentences.zh: 有了代理，我可以用 Rust 重写它，我只是不需要，所以我不这么做。

sentences.en: It doesn’t warrant that level of performance and the business logic is more digestible in its current form.
sentences.zh: 它不能保证当前形式的性能水平和业务逻辑更容易理解。

sentences.en: Given the amount of reviewing I need to do now, readability is worth more than performance.
sentences.zh: 考虑到我现在需要做的审查量，可读性比性能更有价值。

sentences.en: There are always tradeoffs.Are you implying that them attempting a Rust rewrite would fail, and this is why they’re not doing it?
sentences.zh: 总是需要权衡。你是否暗示他们尝试 Rust 重写会失败，这就是他们不这样做的原因？

blocks.html_en: Also famously terrible. If not for the walled garden and the fact that anthropic can take away our ability to use custom harnesses any day, I would not be using CC. Given some of the features they add I've also started to suspect that their models are making product decisions. Not necessarily intentionally, but more in the sense that I think their engineers just let their models build and add whatever it feels like is a good idea to the codebase and then they just publish that.

sentences.en: Also famously terrible.
sentences.zh: 也是出了名的可怕。

sentences.en: If not for the walled garden and the fact that anthropic can take away our ability to use custom harnesses any day, I would not be using CC.
sentences.zh: 如果不是因为围墙花园以及人为因素随时都会剥夺我们使用定制安全带的能力，我就不会使用 CC。

sentences.en: Given some of the features they add I've also started to suspect that their models are making product decisions.
sentences.zh: 鉴于他们添加的一些功能，我也开始怀疑他们的模型正在做出产品决策。

sentences.en: Not necessarily intentionally, but more in the sense that I think their engineers just let their models build and add whatever it feels like is a good idea to the codebase and then they just publish that.
sentences.zh: 不一定是故意的，但更重要的是，我认为他们的工程师只是让他们的模型构建并将任何感觉像是好主意的东西添加到代码库中，然后他们就发布它。

blocks.html_en:
Yes, that's true, but in the post, they even mention that building the spec is the scarce resource.

  For that to work, the swarm has to actually follow the spec, which is what much of this post is about. We gave the swarm 835 pages of prose and it came back with a database. What was scarce in this experiment, and what we expect to be scarce in software engineering going forward, is the right description of intent.

sentences.en: Yes, that's true, but in the post, they even mention that building the spec is the scarce resource.
sentences.zh: 是的，确实如此，但在帖子中，他们甚至提到构建规范是稀缺资源。

sentences.en: For that to work, the swarm has to actually follow the spec, which is what much of this post is about.
sentences.zh: 为了实现这一点，群体必须真正遵循规范，这就是本文的大部分内容。

sentences.en: We gave the swarm 835 pages of prose and it came back with a database.
sentences.zh: 我们给了这群人 835 页的散文，他们带回来了一个数据库。

sentences.en: What was scarce in this experiment, and what we expect to be scarce in software engineering going forward, is the right description of intent.
sentences.zh: 这个实验中所缺乏的，以及我们预计未来软件工程中所缺乏的，是对意图的正确描述。

blocks.html_en: We're rediscovering the "architect" job role a decade or so after switching to agile processes and staff engineers largely replaced it. God help us when they rediscover UML for agents.

sentences.zh: 在转向敏捷流程十年左右后，我们正在重新发现“架构师”工作角色，并且工程师在很大程度上取代了它。

sentences.zh: 当他们重新发现代理的 UML 时，上帝会帮助我们。

blocks.html_en: Easily the biggest delta between a good one and a bad one I've ever encountered. It's so easy to test too. Just ask a product manager if they can give a talk on <some product they own> in 2 hours. If they respond by slacking all of the engineers that work under them to stop what they're doing and give them the info to give that talk, fire them. If they are ready to talk right now then give them a raise.

sentences.en: Easily the biggest delta between a good one and a bad one I've ever encountered.
sentences.zh: 这无疑是我遇到过的好人和坏人之间最大的差异。

sentences.en: It's so easy to test too.
sentences.zh: 测试起来也很容易。

sentences.en: Just ask a product manager if they can give a talk on <some product they own> in 2 hours.
sentences.zh: 只需询问产品经理，他们是否可以在 2 小时内就<他们拥有的某些产品>进行演讲。

sentences.en: If they respond by slacking all of the engineers that work under them to stop what they're doing and give them the info to give that talk, fire them.
sentences.zh: 如果他们的反应是解雇所有在他们手下工作的工程师，以停止他们正在做的事情，并为他们提供演讲所需的信息，那就解雇他们。

sentences.en: If they are ready to talk right now then give them a raise.
sentences.zh: 如果他们现在准备好谈话，那就给他们加薪。

blocks.html_en: Easily the biggest delta between a good one and a bad one I've ever encountered. It's so easy to test too. Just ask a product manager if they can give a talk on <some product they own> in 2 hours. If they respond by slacking all of the engineers that work under them to stop what they're doing and give them the info to give that talk, fire them. If they are ready to talk right now then give them a raise.

blocks.html_en: I mean, how long are these pages? Assuming 40 or so lines per page, that would be 33400 lines of spec turned into 200,000 lines of Rust, or about 6 lines of code per line of spec. That's a very detailed spec, much more than any software specification I have ever written in my career. It's not obvious to me that writing this spec would meaningfully save work over incrementally building individual features, especially when you think about what it would take for human beings to meaningfully review whether those 835 pages contain the right intent.

sentences.en: I mean, how long are these pages?
sentences.zh: 我的意思是，这些页面有多长？

sentences.en: Assuming 40 or so lines per page, that would be 33400 lines of spec turned into 200,000 lines of Rust, or about 6 lines of code per line of spec.
sentences.zh: 假设每页有 40 行左右，那么 33400 行规范就会变成 200,000 行 Rust，或者每行规范大约有 6 行代码。

sentences.en: That's a very detailed spec, much more than any software specification I have ever written in my career.
sentences.zh: 这是一个非常详细的规范，比我职业生涯中写过的任何软件规范都要详细得多。

sentences.en: It's not obvious to me that writing this spec would meaningfully save work over incrementally building individual features, especially when you think about what it would take for human beings to meaningfully review whether those 835 pages contain the right intent.
sentences.zh: 对我来说，编写此规范是否会比逐步构建单个功能有意义地节省工作量并不明显，特别是当您考虑人类需要如何才能有意义地审查这 835 页是否包含正确的意图时。

blocks.html_en: not only that, but what all of this misses is the fact that the specification that they used was a product of writing the software, not the other way round. It would be much, much more difficult to first write a correct specification than to first write a correct program, because programs are formal systems that can be executed to observe their correctness. You can’t do that with a specification.

sentences.en: not only that, but what all of this misses is the fact that the specification that they used was a product of writing the software, not the other way round.
sentences.zh: 不仅如此，所有这些都忽略了这样一个事实：他们使用的规范是编写软件的产物，而不是相反。

sentences.en: It would be much, much more difficult to first write a correct specification than to first write a correct program, because programs are formal systems that can be executed to observe their correctness.

sentences.en: You can’t do that with a specification.

blocks.html_en: Well, the tool works well. I am grateful that Nature has seen fit that the same kind of person who can make it doesn’t also have the ability to identify what needs exist in the market because then all software would flow out of OpenAI and Anthropic and the rest of us would run about their legs and peep about. Whether product development will survive the next generation of models is still unknown.

对于组织来说，你基本上可以投资一个拥有数百台小型计算机的本地服务器。你可以使用工程师和前沿模型来规划和进行任务细分。 然后将其发送给本地集群，本地集群基本上处理每个小任务，但不知道它应该做什么。真正的问题是中间的问题。 测试、验证中级抽象的互连性、以如此高的速度构建大量技术债务，并以任何方式实际了解你所构建的是什么。我认为，对于大多数 MBA 付费而不是为了营销。这很酷，但我更想知道他们最成功的用户在做什么，以及他们如何衡量这一点。> 今年早些时候的浏览器群在 Git 上达到了每小时大约 1,000 次提交的峰值。 新系统的峰值约为每秒 1,000 次提交。>为了促进这一活动速度，我们从头开始构建了一个新的版本控制系统 (VCS)。 吞吐量并不是拥有该层的唯一原因。 系统中的每个更改都会通过 VCS，因此它是冲突首先变得可见的地方，并且下一节中的几个协调机制直接在其内部实现。谈论发明宇宙来制作按钮。制作一个高吞吐量的修订控制系统是可行的，这里已经用 git-in-postgres 等在 HN 上进行了实验。问题是，这里的实际瓶颈是什么？我为 LLM 辅助工作制作了一个 VCS，在开发时用自己的代码引导它。 事实上，如果它没有真正发挥作用，它甚至不会存在！ 根据我的经验，跟踪事物并评估它们是瓶颈，我专注于 https://replicated.live/blog/worktree 实际上，问题是“如何利用人类评估和反馈”。 Cursor 人以“品味、判断和方向来自人类”结束了他们的上一篇文章。 在前面的一些段落中“不要编译它，更快地提交它”之后，听起来令人惊讶地理智。人们经常认为技术/业务是瓶颈问题，即我的限制因素是什么。 这与前人工智能时代的商业相关，因为人类是相对自组织的。人工智能的问题是它没有真正的记忆、背景、长期推理等。我们用这些东西来确定我们做某事的好坏以及原因。可以说，人工智能的“瓶颈”是，它只会永远循环地构建垃圾，无限扩展范围，然后继续构建。最好的情况下，它会自动构建你的独角兽SaaS。 但平均而言，这是一个由不断扩展的代码组成的癌症集群，没有任何计划。无监督人工智能的问题在于，当你增加吞吐量时，你就会减少人类监督的数量。 所以你必须有某种方法来处理这个问题。如果我要给人工智能一个非常复杂的巨大任务，它可能会搞砸数百次，我将如何弥补这一点，而不需要不断地监视它？但这大多只是普通模型。代理开发的一些关键部分实际上已经有几个月的历史了，而大多数成熟的部分只有几年的历史。批判性思维是模型要达到的最关键的检查点。现在正确的工具和编排的出现只是时间问题。 一旦智能体的记忆和回忆运转良好，所有这些瓶颈都会一一消失。老实说，对我来说这似乎非常合理，VCS 的构建是为了促进人类工作结构，重建它以促进自主工作似乎是合适的。这不就是AI的核心基础吗？ 将结果限制在具有所有可能答案的潜在空间，然后根据随机抽样选择有意义的标记？我觉得harness和LLM的整个概念就是选择有意义的随机标记。 将其视为一个解决方案空间（一个非常复杂的空间），然后线束和 LLM 正在朝着代码完成的局部最小值移动。 无论如何，这类似于我们对人工智能的看法。是的，代理编码就是机器学习。 不是因为底层的语言模型，而是因为它是梯度下降，测试工具为代理提供了一个梯度来遍历。如果最终产生了莎士比亚级别的文学，那么我们是否通过随机击键到达那里有什么关系吗？产生了莎士比亚级别的文学。 我们是无限的猴子，其中之一就是莎士比亚。老实说，从他的作品数量来看，有很多。很少会遇到仅仅创作出几件完美作品的杰出艺术家——通常来说，那些几部巨著都是在剪辑室的地板废料上建造的。导演詹姆斯·卡梅隆之所以脱颖而出，是因为他只制作了票房大片。 像你说的那样很难做到，大多数艺术家和导演都会很高兴拥有一件可以被人们记住的伟大作品，即使它是在十几件容易被遗忘的作品中。他只出版过轰动一时的作品。 但我确信他有很多从未见过世面的练习片？正确的。 莎士比亚猴子或博尔赫斯图书馆的笑话是，你只需这样做，然后过滤出与莎士比亚作品匹配的书籍。如果你已经知道你想要在无限图书馆中找到什么，它就在那里。 如果您不确切知道要查找的内容，您可能无法找到它。问题是一路上产生的无数其他文献。 诚然，《神鬼寓言》在更快的时间内达到了“良好”的结果（0.7~）。我很难正确使用《寓言》。 我所做的一切最终都会被标记并恢复到 Opus。 需要做一些挖掘。> 为了测试这一进展，我们回到了旧群体一直在努力解决的一项任务：用 Rust 从头开始​​构建 SQLite，除了它的文档之外什么都没有。 SQLite 的源代码不是在它的训练数据中吗？即使在训练期间没有看到它的 Rust 代码，LLM 也可以轻松地将 SQLite 的 C 代码库即时转译为 Rust。 例如，我只是要求 ChatGPT 用 Erlang 编写 John Carmack 著名的快速反平方根算法，没有在网上搜索或思考，它立即转译了它（同时也在同一步骤中提取了知识）。 SQLite 的语义/代码存储在 LLM 的中间层中，最后一层能够根据提示将其转换为任何表示形式。 Cursor 的实验存在严重缺陷，因为他们只是提取了模型的 SQLite 代码库的压缩、有损知识，然后运行了一系列测试/修复轮次来弥补损失。 关于特工从头开始建造它的说法是错误的。然后您会期望实现的结构与 SQLite 相同，并且浏览了结果，看起来至少有些东西不是这样。 例如，它似乎使用运算符树执行器而不是 SQLite 的字节码解释器。关键是模型的权重中有源代码。 因此，当它可以在 Rust 中生成 SQLite 实现而不需要任何文档时，专注于“仅给出文档”是具有误导性的。 这个措辞的目的是创造一种营销印象，即它可以为一个新颖的项目进行规范驱动的开发，但这个例子实际上并不支持这一点。这就像一个饮食系统，广告发言人在他们的系统上减掉了多少体重，但没有透露他们同时也服用了 Ozempic。知识是有损的，并且代码生成本身是不确定的（温度），因此运算符树执行器必须受到其他数据库实现的干扰，因为字节码解释器并不常见为了测试这个假设，您需要在后续代理开始重构之前查看初始版本。为什么呢？ 代理的全部要点是需要多次传递才能得到结果。 如果步骤 a) 是非惯用的转译，但步骤 b) c) 和... z) 使其变得惯用、高效且高效，为什么要考虑步骤 a)？ 那只是开始。 “代理”和“群”以及动态这个和那个的想法是，您只查看最后一步。这严格遵循早期的代理用法，人们抱怨代码充满错误等。 但是让循环执行计划-写入-验证是让我们从那种状态发展到今天的原因。 它有效。 如果您查看代码并发现其中有错误（linting 等），则说明您的循环设置不正确。 只有当所有事情都可以而且应该过去的时候，你才应该被召唤。 你的角色是“方向/品味/人类常识一览”。因为测试的目的是看看代理群体方法的能力。 如果您的测试假设您尝试构建的内容的版本在训练数据中可用，或者无法确定您正在构建的内容是否在训练数据中是否重要，那么这不是一个非常有用的测试。 大多数时候，当您考虑代理方法时，您并不是试图重建所涉及的代理所接受的培训。Turso 是用 Rust 重写的 sqlite，并带有一些附加功能。 它是开源的，所以我想它会出现在训练数据中。这些测试很有趣，但我不禁觉得这些都是基准测试结果。 “我们让我们的工具在 Rust 中使用 sqlite”听起来比重新创建像 facebook 这样的应用程序要容易得多。 我认为必须整合的概念才是真正的挑战。 基于体面的文档用新语言重写代码是一个困难但不太有用的衡量人工智能如何帮助取代工程师的方法。想象一下：你需要构建一个新的 CRUD 应用程序，但你的人工智能代理花费 2000 万托克来编写它自己的自定义登录功能，这是浪费时间和金钱。 在这种情况下，我不希望有人为我开发新系统。 我认为我们需要一个测试，其中代理的任务更具体，以找到值得集成的技术，从而编写最少的代码行。机器学习的本质是解决易于测量的代理问题，然后对其进行优化。 当您不确定是否可以设计和实现 OSS 项目的重写时，为什么还要担心没有数据的后台集成微服务呢？我们如何知道这些模型是否没有经过 Turso 用 Rust 重写 SQLite 进行训练？它们似乎很可能并且不可能从预训练中删除该代码。 这难道不就是LLM对训练集的记忆吗？ 我缺少什么？> 我错过了什么？他们在同一任务上测试相同的模型，但组织群体的方式不同，新方法效果更好。我希望看到更多的线束工程以代码形式共享。
